transcript:
Q1: Hi Yatharth, it's lovely to meet you!  What first sparked your interest in becoming a caregiver?
A1: Uh yes sir. I have worked for about two years as a home caregiver, supporting mostly seniors and a few post-operative clients. Day-to-day I handled bathing, dressing, light meal prep and medication, and checking vital temperature. I also did housekeeping and simple notes for families.

Q2: That's wonderful experience, Yatharth.  What qualities do you think are most important for a successful caregiver?
A2: Patient reliability for sure. I want someone who communicates clearly and follows the care plan and respects routines. Basics of safety awareness like how to prevent falls and being calm under pressure matters too.

Q3: That's a great list.  What specific skills have you developed during your work?
A3: Yeah, I mean my last role taught me to stay organized and follow instructions from nurses and families. Uh, I learned to prioritize when several tasks compete and to stay calm when a client is having a tough day. Keeping notes improved my communication.

Q4: That's excellent, Yatharth.  What motivates you to be a caregiver?
A4: पीपीपीपी

Q5: That's interesting.  Can you tell me more about what motivates you to be a caregiver?
A5: I like helping people feel safe at home. The best part Yeah, I like helping people feel safe at home. The best part is when a client is comfortable and you can tell you can tell you made their day easier. Uh like when someone says they slept better or enjoyed a meal you made.

Q6: That sounds very rewarding.  Tell me about a time you were late for a shift.
A6: Uh yeah, I was late once because a bus broke down. Uh my client missed a short morning walk that we usually do. I called ahead to let them know and apologized when I arrived. After that, I started leaving earlier and checking transit updates as I left before I left.

Q7: How would you describe your reputation among your previous clients?
A7: Beep beep

Q8: That’s good you took steps to prevent it from happening again.  What would you say are your greatest strengths as a caregiver?
A8: Please

Q9: I understand.  Tell me about a time you had to adapt to a challenging situation with a client.
A9: Yeah, I mean, uh, as one of my greatest skills is basically that I'm patient and reliable.

Q10: That's excellent.  Can you give me an example of a time you had to be patient with a client?
A10: Let's see. I mean, I supported a client with memory issues. The hardest part was answering the same questions. Yeah, basically I had to support a client with memory issues. The hardest part was answering the same questions repeatedly without sounding impatient. I learned to keep a steady routine and use simple reassuring answers. The meaningful part was seeing her relax and smile more over time.

Q11: That's wonderful; it sounds like you built a strong rapport with her.  What would you say are your three greatest strengths as a caregiver?
A11: I mean, I would say dependable, dependable, polite and patient.

Q12: Dependable, polite, and patient are all excellent qualities.  What would your references say about your work ethic?
A12: Again they would say that I'm reliable and dependable.

Q13: Thanks for taking the time to interview, please leave the call by clicking the end interview button on your screen, we will reach out to you if you are a good fit.
A13: [No response recorded]
